top_signals.title: The Iran Stalemate Could Be the Next Oil Supercycle Trigger
top_signals.summary: As one deadline for a peace deal in the U.S./Israel-Iran war passes another, the chance of no firm agreement being struck to end the conflict in the coming months is rising dramatically. There are good reasons why Washington under President Donald Trump may be perfectly happy to keep this conflict simmering as it is, including the effective closure of the world’s critical transit route, the Strait of Hormuz. There are also good reasons why Tehran under the Islamic Revolutionary Guards Corps (IRGC) might wish to do the same. So, it may be…
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: VPS: Fuel Quality Management for Vessels in Extended Idle
top_signals.summary: Since the closure of the Strait of Hormuz to most commercial shipping in late February 2026, an estimated 1,550 vessels, carrying approximately 20,000 seafarers, have been unable to transit, or have chosen to remain at anchor in the Arabian Gulf, Gulf of Oman, and approaches.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Inflation expectations impacting rate path, bonds and global risk assets.

top_signals.title: Spot Bitcoin ETFs bleed $1.7B as outflow streak hits four weeks
top_signals.summary: BlackRock’s IBIT accounted for most of the weekly Bitcoin ETF redemptions, while Fidelity and Grayscale funds also saw outflows.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Digital asset volatility and institutional crypto positioning changes.

top_signals.title: Bitcoin price eyes $90K as FTX-era BTC bullish divergence flashes again
top_signals.summary: Bitcoin flashes only its second weekly bullish divergence on record, a signal that previously preceded a 755% BTC price rally.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Digital asset volatility and institutional crypto positioning changes.
